description: Uplifting, powerful anthem about overcoming obstacles and rising stronger, perfect for montages and personal empowerment
target_audience: general, athletes, students, anyone needing inspiration
suno_tags: inspirational anthem, uplifting pop rock, powerful vocals, driving drums, piano and guitar, triumphant, 120 BPM
vocal_style: Starts grounded and determined in the verses, rises with conviction in the pre-chorus, and explodes into full-powered, arms-wide-open delivery in the chorus. The voice carries both struggle and triumph — you hear the journey in the tone.

tips: Verse = the struggle (falling, doubt, darkness). Chorus = the triumph (rising, strength, light)
Use physical action words: stand, rise, run, fight, climb, break through
Chorus should work as a standalone affirmation — tattoo-worthy lines
Bridge is the quiet moment of resolve before the final powerful chorus
Write for someone who needs to hear this on their hardest day

example_lyrics:
[Intro]
[Grand Piano]

[Verse 1]
I've been down on the ground
Tasting dirt between my teeth
Every voice said stay down
But something stirred beneath

[Pre-Chorus]
I'm not done, I'm not broken
I'm just getting to my feet

[Chorus]
[Powerful]
I will rise above the wreckage
I will carry every scar
Every fall just built the fire
Look how far I've come, look how far

[Verse 2]
Rain came down and washed the road
Blurred the lines I used to see
But I found my way through flooded miles
And the strongest version of me

[Pre-Chorus]
I'm not lost, I'm not shattered
I'm becoming who I need to be

[Chorus]
[Belted]
I will rise above the wreckage
I will carry every scar
Every fall just built the fire
Look how far I've come, look how far

[Bridge]
[Soft]
There will be nights you can't see morning
And the silence feels like weight
But the dawn has never failed to come
And neither will your strength

[Chorus]
[Powerful]
I will rise above the wreckage
I will carry every scar
Every fall just built the fire
Look how far I've come, look how far

[Outro]
Look how far
[Instrumental]
[End]
do: Contrast struggle (verses) with triumph (chorus) — the arc is everything
Use 'I' in verses for personal journey, shift to 'we'/'you' in bridge for universality
Build dynamics from piano intro to full band finale
Make the chorus a complete statement of empowerment in 4 lines
Bridge should speak directly to the listener — 'you' language
dont: Don't be preachy or lecture — inspire through story, not instruction
Don't use vague platitudes like 'believe in yourself' — be specific
Don't keep the energy flat — motivational songs need a clear arc upward
Don't skip the struggle — triumph means nothing without the valley
Don't end on doubt — the final chorus must be the most powerful delivery
